parsed_review.summary: CP00-505 is a clean, well-formed descriptor-only governance pack covering exactly the 40 planned RP16 units RP16.P07.M02.S17-RP16.P07.M04.S12 (the M02 Type And Shape Definition tail of 6 rows, the full M03 Primary Implementation Slice of 22 rows, and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head of 12 rows). Scope, risk class (B), unit count (40), and the deliverable distribution (test=4, security_audit=4, hermes_evidence=3, claude_review=2, implementation=3, failure_recovery=23, fixture=1 -> 40) and micro-title/micro-phase distributions (Type And Shape Definition=6 / Primary Implementation Slice=22 / Secondary Workflow Slice=12; M02=6/M03=22/M04=12) match exactly across the review baseline included_units, prompt, registry GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP505_REQUIREMENTS, manifest delivery_counts, and the contract p07_implementation_slice_descriptor projection. The handoff chain CP00-504->CP00-505->CP00-506 with next subphase RP16.P07.M04.S13 is consistent end-to-end (pack binding, contract, README, handoff builder). All seven required public exports exist and are reachable through the index.js barrel: createGovernanceCoreCp505P07ImplementationSliceCaseSet/Descriptor (service.js), validateGovernanceCoreCp505Coverage, validateGovernanceCoreCp505P07ImplementationSliceDescriptor, createGovernanceCoreCp505HermesEvidencePacket/ClaudeReviewPacket/CloseoutHandoff (validators.js). The pack does NOT overclaim production readiness: manifest and command-evidence keep production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, and the sole flag (governance_core_p07_implementation_slice_descriptor_verified) is a descriptor-verification flag supported by the service descriptor/case-set builders, three CP505 unit tests, the H16 deterministic Hermes evidence packet, the validate-rp16-governance-core-contract.mjs deepEqual drift checks, and the passing local validation commands (all exit_code 0, plus a passing auth preflight returning valid non-zero-byte JSON). Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries are structurally enforced at every layer (freezeCp505Result + CP505 row builder: descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false; synthetic_only/no_real_data true; all dispatches_*/writes_*/creates_database_rows/emits_hermes_runtime_receipt/exposes_* flags false). Claude is held read-only and explicitly non-final-approver (request tools Read,Grep,Glob only; authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false; promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true), and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt handling (receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. CP178+ review-hardening forbidden_review_evidence guards (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, missing references/fields, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim) and the no-leak guards are present and inherited from the CP480 baseline. Unrelated dirty/untracked files (progress-control-room data/html, workbook docs, docx, LDIP review result) are correctly classified as preserved and out of pack scope in changed-file-scope.json, with active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty. Post-review artifacts (raw-output.json, review-receipt.json, claude-review-result.json) are not yet present, which is the expected state at this read-only review step of the gated 9-step hardening sequence, not a defect. No P0-P3 findings; the pack is suitable to proceed to the remaining hardening/closeout steps under human final approval.
